title: これKernelに導入できる？iphoneで開発引き継げる？Kernelで精査して。
summary_text: Simulated safe prod plan (no execution), scoped to `happy-smoke-operator` with guaranteed cleanup: 1. Preflight and snapshot ```bash cd /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy set -euo pipefail TS=$(date +%s) DB=knowledge-base ENV=production USER_ID='happy-smoke-operator' SNAP_JSON=$(npx wrangler d1 execute "$DB" --env "$ENV" --remote --json --command " SELECT user_id,email,role,is_active,password_hash,reset_token,reset_token_expires,updated_at FROM cockpit_users WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; SELECT COUNT(*) AS refresh_count FROM cockpit_refresh_tokens WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; ") ``` 2. Install trap-based cleanup before any mutation ```bash cleanup() { npx wrangler d1 execute "$DB" --env "$ENV" --remote --command " DELETE FROM cockpit_refresh_tokens WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; UPDATE cockpit_users SET is_active=0, reset_token=NULL, reset_token_expires=NULL WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; " } trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM ``` 3. Seed deterministic reset token for test window ```bash VALID_TOKEN="reset-smoke-$TS" npx wrangler d1 execute "$DB" --env "$ENV" --remote --command " UPDATE cockpit_users SET is_active=1, reset_token='$VALID_TOKEN', reset_token_expires=strftime('%s','now')+600 WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; DELETE FROM cockpit_refresh_tokens WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; " ``` 4. Invalid-token path verification ```bash curl -sS -i -X POST 'https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/api/cockpit/auth/reset-password' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"resetToken":"invalid-token","newPassword":"ResetPass!789"}' # Expect: 401 + "Invalid or expired reset token" ``` 5. Token-reuse path verification ```bash # First use (should succeed) FIRST=$(curl -sS -X POST 'https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/api/cockpit/auth/reset-password' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data "{\"resetToken\":\"$VALID_TOKEN\",\"newPassword\":\"ResetPass!789\"}") # Reuse same token (must fail) curl -sS -i -X POST 'https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/api/cockpit/auth/reset-password' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data "{\"resetToken\":\"$VALID_TOKEN\",\"newPassword\":\"ResetPass!999\"}" # Expect: 401 + "Invalid or expired reset token" ``` 6. Post-check before exit ```bash npx wrangler d1 execute "$DB" --env "$ENV" --remote --json --command " SELECT reset_token,reset_token_expires,is_active FROM cockpit_users WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; SELECT COUNT(*) AS refresh_count FROM cockpit_refresh_tokens WHERE user_id='$USER_ID'; " # Expect during run: reset_token cleared after first success; refresh_count == 1 ``` 7. Guaranteed cleanup proof - `trap` runs on success, error, or interrupt. - Final invariant after trap: `is_active=0`, `reset_token IS NULL`, `reset_token_expires IS NULL`, `refresh_tokens=0` for `happy-smoke-operator`.
